thread.title: Female Voter Turnout Sees Historic Surge in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar | Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar News - Times of India
thread.title_full: Female Voter Turnout Sees Historic Surge in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar | Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar News - Times of India

author: Prasad Joshi
title: Female Voter Turnout Sees Historic Surge in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar | Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar News - Times of India
text: Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar: The district registered over 6% rise in female voter turnout during the current assembly polls as compared to 2019 elections.A rise of 2% in polling percentage of male voters was also registered in the district, which recorded an overall voter turnout of 69.5%, showing a surge of 5% compared to the previous assembly polls, official data said.District collector Deelip Swami said various awareness drives involving female voters resulted in more women coming out to cast their votes.\"We started our drives for assembly polls soon after the Lok Sabha election. We took help from various self-help groups (SHGs) to reach out to voters with an appeal for voting. A series of special drives were also arranged through Mahila Arthik Vikas Mahamandal (State Women's Development Corporation) to sensitise female voters. Workshops were also held targeting working women,\" he said.Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar had 13.48 lakh female voters during the 2019 assembly polls, and the number increased by nearly two lakh to 15.39 lakh during 2024 assembly elections, according to official data.Leaders from MahaYuti said that increased female voter turnout was the effect of ‘Mukhyamantri Ladki Bahin' scheme of the state govt that offers target women beneficiaries a monthly aid of Rs 1,500.\"The scheme gained unprecedented popularity, and many beneficiaries knew that it would be discontinued if the MVA came to power. Therefore, those beneficiaries came out in large numbers to vote in support of our scheme,\" he said.Congress spokesperson Pawan Dongare, however, denounced such claims. \"The MVA also announced Maha Laxmi scheme for women, offering a monthly dole of Rs 3,000. The Maha Yuti leaders are erring while looking at the election data, and they will realise it on the day of election results,\" he said.When asked about the possible effect of ‘Ladki Bahin' scheme on female voter turnout, Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar district collector refused comment. \"We can only speak about our efforts towards increasing voter turnout,\" he said.
